title: WHATWG-Informed HTML Trees, Cheerio, parse5, and Parser Differentials · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Cheerio tree-construction evidence adapter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Distinguish source tokens, parse5-repaired tree, Cheerio selections, browser DOM, accessibility tree, and rendered state.
Choose document, fragment, HTML, or XML loading options deliberately and test wrapper insertion and case behavior.
Test misnesting, omitted elements, tables, templates, foreign content, duplicate attributes, and foster parenting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Tree Budget
steps.instruction:
Order the Tree Budget Authorized site auditing and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Tree Budget, keep Tree Budget connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 3173 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Cheerio loading modes, malformed tree repair, parser differentials, budgets, and source-tree distinctions; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound source, nodes, depth, attributes, text, selector work, and parse duration and expose rejection causes.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Tree Budget
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-tree-budget-129evm" that uses case 2137 to prove Cheerio loading modes, malformed tree repair, parser differentials, budgets, and source-tree distinctions. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
